issues: outline_treatment: candidate lacks clear 1px black outlines present on all peer tiles; edges blend into background or use dark-green self-color
outline_color: peers use pure black separators; candidate uses dark teal/green border with no black perimeter
shadow_direction: peers have zero or minimal implicit shadows embedded in tile shading; candidate has an explicit dark oval drop-shadow beneath the circle — stylistically alien
shadow_count: candidate introduces a dedicated shadow layer absent from any peer tile
highlight_intensity: candidate has high-intensity white petal/sparkle shapes radiating outward — far brighter and more decorative than any highlight present in the dungeon tile peers
color_temperature: peers are cool-neutral (gray stone, dark teal, muted red); candidate introduces warm pink/rose orb as primary focal color — warm hue not represented in peer palette
detail_density: circular layered composition with inner orb + wreath ring + petal highlights + blue sparkles at four corners is significantly denser than any single peer tile

rendering_style: peers use flat limited-palette pixel art with at most 2-3 shades per tile; candidate exhibits gradient-like shading within the teal ring and the pink orb has smooth radial lightness falloff inconsistent with peer rendering method
palette_overlap: teal is shared, but pink/rose, pure white petals, and sky-blue corner sparkles have no equivalent in peer palette; overlap is partial at best

charm_level: peers are functional grim dungeon tiles (stone, blood, bones, machinery); candidate is a soft floral summon mandala with a cute pink orb — charm register is dramatically higher and mismatched to the peer family aesthetic
proportion: circular organic silhouette vs rectilinear grid-aligned tile shapes — not penalized as item-type difference, but contributes to overall cohesion failure
reasoning: Across 9 of 11 evaluated dimensions the candidate diverges materially from the Denzi dungeon-tile peer set. The rendering method alone is disqualifying: peers are flat pixel art; the candidate uses gradient-like internal shading that reads as a different engine or artist pass. The pink/rose primary color is entirely absent from the peer palette. The explicit drop-shadow oval is a compositional device with no peer equivalent. Most critically, the charm-level gap is severe — the floral summon mandala reads as a different game's asset placed alongside grim stone tiles. Only the teal hue and pixel-art medium create any family resemblance. Score 4 reflects two partial passes (teal color overlap, shared pixel-art medium) against nine dimension failures.
